proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5  (Semantic Correctness – non‑negative coverage counts)
; --------------------------------------------------------------
; Model one iteration of the aggregation loop in runReport:
;   totalProven := totalProvenPrev + c.proven.length
; Both operands are natural numbers (length of an array cannot be negative).
; Prove that the resulting totalProven can never be negative.
(declare-const totalProvenPrev Int)   ; value before the current contract is added
(declare-const provenLen       Int)   ; c.proven.length for the current contract
(declare-const totalProven     Int)   ; value after the addition

; Preconditions established by the language runtime / TypeScript semantics:
;   * Array lengths are ≥ 0
;   * The accumulator starts at 0, therefore stays ≥ 0 after each iteration
(assert (>= totalProvenPrev 0))
(assert (>= provenLen 0))

; Transition (the actual code mutation):
(assert (= totalProven (+ totalProvenPrev provenLen)))

; Negated property we want to disprove:
;   totalProven < 0  (i.e. a negative coverage count)
(assert (< totalProven 0))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat  → the property “totalProven is never negative” is guaranteed.
